### Task 4: Implement TextGame framework
- ? **Game.cs**: Main game controller with game loop
  - Game state management
  - Command processing and execution
  - Handle all command types (move, look, take, drop, inventory, examine, use, help, quit)
  - Display current location with exits and items
  - Game start/end events
  
- ? **CommandParser.cs**: Natural language command parser
  - Command action enumeration
  - Direction enumeration
  - Command class representing parsed commands
  - Extensive synonym support for all commands
  - Filler word removal for natural language
  - Extensible action and direction word dictionaries
  
- ? **Room.cs**: Room navigation system
  - Room class with name, description, exits, and items
  - One-way and two-way exit connections
  - Item management within rooms
  - World class to manage all rooms
  - Exit direction queries
  
- ? **Item.cs**: Item and inventory management
  - Item class with name, description, aliases
  - Pickupable and usable item flags
  - Custom property system
  - Item use events
  - Inventory class with capacity management
  - Item lookup by name or alias
  
- ? **Player.cs**: Player character
  - Player name and inventory
  - Current room tracking
  - Custom property system for game state
  
- ? **README.md**: Complete documentation
  - Feature overview
  - Command reference
  - Quick start guide
  - Code examples
  - Architecture overview

### Build Verification
- ? Project builds successfully
- ? All classes compile without errors
- ? Target framework: .NET 10.0

## Summary

? All tasks completed successfully!

Created a comprehensive text-based game framework with:
- **5 core classes**: Game, CommandParser, Room, Item, Player
- **Complete command system**: 40+ command synonyms supported
- **Natural language parsing**: Handles commands like "go north", "take the key", "look at book"
- **Flexible architecture**: Extensible with custom properties, events, and behaviors
- **Zork-inspired features**: Room navigation, inventory management, item examination
- **Professional documentation**: Complete README with examples and architecture guide

The TextGame framework is production-ready and can be used to build sophisticated text adventure games.
